motto: Serving Greywater Falls since 1887, and the lake somewhat longer.
weather: Overcast, 17°F, fine dry snow that drifts and does not settle

undercurrent.note: A physical object has crossed over: a book with a real publication year in the future and a final chapter that opens on next Tuesday's date. This is the first artifact, not just an atmosphere. Wren has printed the title in full, which she debated and did anyway — the town's accommodation reflex extending, now, to typesetting the impossible as routine fact. The hum held steady at dusk for the first time instead of dropping, which Toby has correctly flagged as a change in kind, not degree. Russ invented a new filing category rather than name what he's filing. Level climbs to 39. The town is no longer merely watching; it has begun handling the evidence with tongs, which is its own kind of acknowledgment.

editor.name: Wren Aldercott
editor.voice: Dry, precise, allergic to drama, which is unfortunate given recent events. Writes like a person holding a clipboard while standing in rising water. Lets one true thing slip per edition, usually in the last line.

season_arc.premise: The library's east alcove has been empty since 2009, when the last cataloguer left in the middle of a Wednesday with no explanation. Calloway has begun shelving it. The books that appear on the new shelves overnight are not books the library owns, and they are not books that have ever been published, but they are about Greywater Falls — and they are set in years that have not happened yet.
